title: The Competency Crisis: Why Nobody Seems to Know How to Do Their Jobs Anymore
content:
One of the themes of late in our daily newsletter has been what I've taken to calling the Competency Crisis. Think of the Competency Crisis as a kind of skeleton key to understand this bizarre moment in American politics. Everything seems downstream of the fact that no one seems to know how to do their jobs anymore.

This becomes apparent in your day-to-day life when you start to look for it. No recent event brought it home quite like the thwarted assassination attempt of President Donald Trump. I was stoked when I got an invite to the so-called nerdprom this year, knowing Trump was planning on attending. Say what you will about the man, but he's funny. And this dinner is supposed to be about laughs.

Thankfully, our latest alleged wannabe assassin was just an old-fashioned American weirdo armed with a shotgun. All this is to say, the Secret Service dropped the ball again. Their agents are some of our finest, dealing with a constantly shifting threat matrix in an age of heightened political violence. But its leadership is clearly, deeply incompetent.

Three thwarted attempts on the president's life in two years does not make me feel confident in the agency to protect the homeland. But everyone just shrugs it off. Even Trump is oddly sanguine about the attempts on his life, saying that he was honored because it's only the impactful presidents that people try to kill. The head of the Secret Service has overseen several security lapses now, from the embarrassing to the downright frightening. Why does he still have a job?

The Competency Crisis is everywhere. It's how a Black Hawk pilot found herself flying directly into a commercial airline full of passengers above the Potomac. It's utilities being unable to keep their power lines from starting wildfires. It's baked into the government being unable to effectively wield power when they find themselves entrusted with it. A return to competence would make a powerful political message for the future.
